topic: Simple Boolean logic (AND, OR, XOR, NOT)

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.explanation: B A is true. B is "not A" meaning B is false. Now. !A && (A || B) ^ B First, substitute all the values !true && (true || false) ^ false Next, evaluate !true false && (true || false) ^ false Now, do the parenthesis false && true ^ false Next is "xor" false && true Using and false is the final answer

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.explanation: A A is not true, or false. B is not A, or true A && B is false (two trues are needed) So, C is false. A ^ B ^ C becomes false ^ true ^ false Work left to right keeping in mind that ^ needs a true and a false to be true. false ^ true ^ false true ^ false = true

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.explanation: A boolean A = true; A is true boolean B = !A; B is false boolean C = A || B; C is true (Or needs at least 1 true) boolean D = B && C; D is false (And need 2 trues) boolean E = A ^ D; E is true (Xor needs exactly 1 true) out.print(E);

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?

questions.stem: What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: B (a ^ b) is true XOR false = true, !(b || c) is !(false || true) = !true = false, so the left side of || is true && false = false, but the right side (a && c) is true && true = true, and since false || true = true, result is true.

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.explanation: A int A = 4; int B = 7; int C = 2; boolean D = (A>C) && (B<A); D = false boolean E = (A + B == 10)||(A >= C*2); E = true boolean F = D ^ E; XOR F = true out.print(F);
